content:
# Credit Dispute Intake Quick Reference

## Accepted Dispute Reasons
Use this reference when categorizing a credit-card billing error or transaction dispute for Northstar Bank. The permitted dispute reason values are: unauthorized_fraudulent_charge, duplicate_charge, incorrect_amount, goods_services_not_received, goods_services_not_as_described, canceled_subscription_still_charging, refund_never_processed. Do not translate a customer's description into a reason outside this set. If the facts do not clearly match one of the listed reasons, ask a targeted follow-up before intake continues.

## Intake Information
Capture every required intake value before submitting the dispute record: transaction_id, card_action, card_last_4_digits, full_name, user_id, phone, email, address, contacted_merchant, purchase_date, issue_noticed_date, dispute_reason, resolution_requested, eligible_for_provisional_credit. The customer's narrative should support the selected dispute reason, the requested resolution, and whether the merchant was contacted. Purchase date and issue-noticed date should be recorded separately when both are available.

## Card Status Decision
Apply the card action rule after the dispute reason and any compromise or reissue request facts are known.

1. Cancel and reissue the card if any of these conditions apply: dispute_reason == unauthorized_fraudulent_charge, card_compromised == true, user_requests_reissue == true.
2. Otherwise, leave the card action as keep_active.

For detailed handling of the card decision, see "Internal: Credit Card Dispute Card Action Policy". For timing questions and customer explanations, see "Credit Dispute Timing Faq".
title: Credit Dispute Intake Quick Reference
